questions.question: What is the primary function of the Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) within a Central Processing Unit (CPU)?
options: To manage memory access and control I/O operations.
To decode instructions and sequence micro-operations.
To perform arithmetic calculations and bitwise logical operations.
To store program instructions and data temporarily.
To predict the outcome of conditional branches.

questions.question: In the physical design phase of an Integrated Circuit (IC) development, what is the main objective of the "Floorplanning" stage?
options: To convert the RTL design into a gate-level netlist using logic synthesis.
To verify the design's functional correctness against its specification.
To optimize the clock tree network for minimal skew and latency.
To define the approximate placement, dimensions, and interconnection routes for major functional blocks.
To extract parasitic capacitances and resistances from the layout.

questions.question: In modern superscalar processors employing out-of-order execution, what is the primary benefit achieved by "Register Renaming"?
options: It reduces the number of general-purpose registers required by the Instruction Set Architecture (ISA).
It allows the processor to fetch and decode more instructions simultaneously from the instruction cache.
It eliminates Write-After-Write (WAW) and Write-After-Read (WAR) hazards, enabling greater instruction-level parallelism.
It improves cache hit rates by dynamically remapping memory addresses to physical cache lines.
It simplifies the hardware implementation of branch prediction logic by abstracting register usage.

questions.question: At advanced technology nodes (e.g., 22nm and below), what is the primary architectural advantage of using FinFET transistors over traditional planar MOSFETs in Integrated Circuit (IC) design?
options: Significantly lower manufacturing cost due to simpler fabrication processes.
Enhanced breakdown voltage and power handling for high-power applications.
Improved electrostatic control over the channel, leading to reduced leakage current and better switching performance.
Increased density of memory cells, enabling larger on-chip cache sizes.
Elimination of the need for oxide isolation between devices, saving silicon area.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a JTAG (Joint Test Action Group) interface, commonly referred to as a debug port, in modern System-on-Chip (SoC) designs?
options: To provide a high-speed interface for data transfer between different processing cores on the SoC.
To enable in-system debugging, boundary-scan testing, and in-field programming of the device.
To regulate the power supply voltages for various functional blocks across the chip.
To perform dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) for power optimization.
To act as the primary interface for connecting external peripherals like USB or Ethernet.
